reviews.exp_tables[075]: curve and 400 cap applied to every era per decision 6 - OpenMU flattens one curve onto 0.75/0.95d whose historical caps were lower; accepted as shipped data
reviews.game_config[075].review: exp_jitter_percent: OpenMU uniform-jitter mechanism and 0.8-1.2 values, no classic corroboration - pending classic GS verification; personal_store: ~1.0-era feature, curated backport per decision 1
reviews.game_config[075].drops.review: all rates are OpenMU defaults (categories 0.5/0.3/0.001/0.0001 from GameConfigurationInitializerBase; skill 50% from DefaultDropGenerator) - authentic model is per-monster ItemRate/MoneyRate (Monster.txt) x global ItemDropRate/ZenDropRate (CommonServer.cfg); replace when classic sources land
reviews.game_config[075].option_roll.review: the three drop-time chances and the 2-excellent cap are OpenMU initializer defaults pending authentic classic sources; max_dropped_option_level 3 is commonly held classic knowledge stated here as policy

gaps.exp_tables.formula: v1 formula string killed; curve content is the ExpTable doc comment, table stays authoritative
gaps.max_option_level: jewel ceiling +16 -> item-options domain (not this file)
gaps.prevent_experience_overflow/area_skill_h: deleted (fixed classic rules / typed combat context)
gaps.character_name_regex/max_characters_per_: deleted (host parse-boundary / no Account aggregate in core)
gaps.durability economy (2000/100000/10000): deleted (OpenMU accumulator wear model; W-CMB sources fresh)

notes: exp table trimmed 402 -> 400 entries (index = level-1, levels 1..=400); OpenMU's two padding cells (index-0 and the +1 read guard) have no v2 reader
v1 spec sample total_exp_by_level level 3 = 396 was a typo; authentic value 440 (formula-correct)
tick_duration_ms 100 is a mu-core decision (4), not an OpenMU value
zen_caps 2000000000 override decision 7; OpenMU source has int.MaxValue
nested drops/option_roll sections carry review only, no source_version (provenance is the enclosing record's source_version)
option_roll shape is options-owned, drops shape is drops-owned; this extractor owns the game_config.json envelope/record around them

second_wing_bonus_roll_per_10000 and dinorant_option_roll_per_10000 retired (W-CRAFT): both were consumer-less chaos-machine chances shadowing their authoritative homes — the chaos_mixes WingEconomics record's luck/excellent chances and the dinorant family facts in the craft service; one number, one home
